decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly set up a powerful combo using cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Omniscience to cheat big creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo pieces. By utilizing powerful ramp cards like Lotus Petal and City of Traitors, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats early in the game to secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combo of Show and Tell to cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play early on, often winning the game in a single turn. It includes cards like Sneak Attack and Omniscience to further support this strategy, along with control elements like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect the combo. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with massive threats, making it a high-risk, high-reward deck in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on quickly getting out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier by utilizing Show and Tell and Sneak Attack to cheat them into play. The deck also includes ways to generate mana quickly with cards like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, allowing for explosive plays. Protection is provided through counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats early in the game, bypassing traditional creature summoning methods.

The deck focuses on locking down opponents through a combination of artifacts that disrupt their game plan and control elements that limit their options. It utilizes powerful artifacts like Urza's Tower, Candelabra of Tawnos, and Karn, the Great Creator to establish a lock on the board. Cards like Kozilek's Command and Ugin, Eye of the Storms provide additional disruption, while Trinisphere and Planar Nexus help slow down the opponent's plays. The deck aims to control the game until it can drop a powerful finisher like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or The One Ring to close out the game.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combo of Aluren and Acererak the Archlich to create a powerful and efficient win condition. By playing cards like Show and Tell, Force of Will, and Omniscience, the deck aims to quickly set up the combo and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes cards like Stock Up, Veil of Summer, and Lotus Petal to help accelerate and protect the combo. The overall strategy is to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent by efficiently utilizing the combo pieces to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful artifact cards to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Pithing Needle, it aims to shut down key cards and abilities while generating advantage with cards like Kozilek's Command and Planar Nexus. The deck also includes elements of counterplay with cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of artifact synergy and disruption, ultimately securing victory through strategic plays and resource management.

This deck focuses on aggressively applying pressure to the opponent while disrupting their game plan with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares. The deck also utilizes powerful equipment like Batterskull and Kaldra Compleat to further enhance its creatures. In addition, it has a land destruction theme with cards like Wasteland and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth. The deck aims to quickly end the game by overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures and removal spells, while also maintaining control of the game through disruption and resource denial.

This deck aims to quickly disrupt the opponent's mana base by utilizing land destruction spells like Magus of the Moon and Sundering Eruption. It also uses aggressive creatures like Pyrogoyf and Broadside Bombardiers to apply pressure on the opponent. The deck includes cards like The One Ring and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker to further enhance its aggressive strategy. By utilizing cards like Ancient Tomb and Chrome Mox for fast mana acceleration, the deck aims to quickly overpower the opponent and win the game before they can effectively respond.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful artifact cards and aggressive creatures to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It utilizes cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Chalice of the Void to control the board state and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, Thundertrap Trainer and Kozilek's Command provide additional ways to apply pressure and deal damage. The deck aims to quickly establish a strong board presence and use powerful spells like Ugin, Eye of the Storms to finish off the opponent. Overall, the key concept is to use aggressive artifact creatures to dominate the battlefield and secure victory.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with cards like Gaea's Cradle and mana dorks like Birds of Paradise, while disrupting the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Collector Ouphe. It also includes a toolbox of creatures like Endurance and Wight of the Reliquary to control the board and respond to threats. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent in the mid to late game, using powerful threats like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Craterhoof Behemoth to close out the game.

Utilize various control elements and card selection to quickly assemble the necessary combo pieces required to win the game with a Thassa's Oracle win condition. The deck focuses on efficiently casting Doomsday and using the powerful card to set up the perfect five-card pile that will result in a win. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides additional card selection, while Murktide Regent can provide a strong alternate win condition if needed. The deck includes a variety of disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo and counter opponent threats.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through a combination of card advantage, disruption, and efficient removal spells. The deck aims to outvalue opponents with cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Quantum Riddler, while also disrupting their plans with counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation. The deck also utilizes versatile removal like Swords to Plowshares and Terminus to deal with threats. Additionally, the deck has a mix of threats like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf to close out the game once it has gained control.

The primary strategy of this deck is to establish a lock on the game by using powerful artifacts to control the board and prevent opponents from gaining momentum. By utilizing cards like Ugin, Karn, and Trinisphere, the deck aims to shut down the opponent's options and create a favorable board state. Additionally, the inclusion of artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Grim Monolith allows for mana acceleration and flexibility in playing powerful spells. Overall, the deck focuses on establishing a prison-like control over the game while gradually building towards a win condition with cards like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.

The key concept of this Legacy Combo deck is to quickly assemble and execute the "Oops, All Spells!" combo, which aims to win the game in a single explosive turn without any lands in the deck. By using cards like Balustrade Spy, Undercity Informer, and Reanimate, the deck aggressively mills through its library to put Thassa's Oracle on the battlefield for a win condition. The deck includes various acceleration cards like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide to quickly and efficiently cast spells, while also utilizing disruption cards like Cabal Therapy and Pact of Negation to protect the combo.

The main objective of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. It focuses on deploying low-cost creatures such as Sand Scout and Amped Raptor early on, supported by powerful buffs from cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory. By utilizing cards like Swords to Plowshares to remove threats and Static Prison to control the board, while also taking advantage of lands like Wasteland for disruption and Plateau for mana fixing, this deck aims to quickly pressure the opponent and secure a victory within a few turns.

This deck focuses on locking down the opponent's resources with powerful artifacts while building up its own mana base. By using cards like Urza's Tower, Grim Monolith, and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck can quickly ramp up and control the game. The main win condition is The One Ring, which can create overwhelming board presence, alongside powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. With a mix of control elements and powerful late-game threats, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and secure victory through overwhelming advantage.
